question_title: Will NASA’s Northrop Grumman CRS-24 mission launch before May 1, 2026?
summary: CRS-24 is scheduled for NET April 8, 2026, with 23 days of margin before the May 1 deadline. It launches on SpaceX Falcon 9 Block 5 (the most reliable operational rocket) from SLC-40 at Cape Canaveral. NASA invited media on March 5-6, indicating final preparations are underway. No delays or issues have been reported. The previous Cygnus XL mission (NG-23) launched successfully in September 2025. For this to resolve NO, the mission would need a 3+ week delay from its target date — an uncommon event for high-priority ISS resupply missions on proven vehicles with no known issues. Potential risks (Falcon 9 grounding, spacecraft anomaly) are low probability and typically resolve within 1-2 weeks, well within the buffer window.

factors.description: Specific launch date set (April 8) with 23 days of margin before May 1 deadline
Falcon 9 Block 5 is the most reliable operational launch vehicle with extremely high success rate and rapid turnaround from scrubs
NASA already invited media (March 5-6 press release) and no delays reported — mission is in final preparations
ISS cargo resupply missions are high priority and rarely experience multi-week delays once a firm date is set
Risk: Falcon 9 fleet-wide grounding could cause delay (has happened historically, typically resolved in 1-2 weeks)
Risk: Late-discovered spacecraft issue could cause delay, though Cygnus XL flew successfully on NG-23
Artemis II launching April 1 from different pad (39B vs SLC-40) — minimal conflict risk

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.95 (95% YES)**

Key reasoning:
- **Launch date**: April 8, 2026 (NET), confirmed by NASA's event page and multiple tracking sites
- **Buffer**: 23 days between scheduled launch and May 1 deadline — only a very significant delay could push past the deadline
- **Vehicle reliability**: Falcon 9 Block 5 has an outstanding track record; even scrubs typically reschedule within days
- **Mission readiness**: NASA invited media in early March, and no delays or issues have been reported
- **NO scenario requires**: A 3+ week delay from the target date, which would need something like a prolonged Falcon 9 grounding or major spacecraft anomaly — both low-probability events that historically resolve within the available buffer
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will NASA’s Northrop Grumman CRS-24 mission launch before May 1, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42925)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if NASA records CRS-24 as launched before May 1, 2026. It resolves to no otherwise. The primary source is NASA’s CRS-24 event page at https://www.nasa.gov/event/nasas-northrop-grumman-crs-24/ Question may also resolve according to  [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions)
fine_print: Use NASA’s own launch record or event page status. A scrub or attempt that does not achieve liftoff does not count. Candidate Sources: NASA news release on CRS-24 launch https://www.nasa.gov/news-release/nasa-invites-media-to-northrop-grumman-crs-24-station-resupply-launch/ ; NASA events page https://www.nasa.gov/events/
